commonIssues:
Despite paying fees, hundreds of thousands of applicants wait months for their smart cards, often receiving non-chipped, laminated cards instead.

How to fix: Immediately link your DL to DigiLocker or mParivahan. Since physical cards are critically delayed due to chip shortages, Traffic Police across India are directed to accept the DigiLocker version.

Karnataka's PUCC data is not properly synced with the central VAHAN system, leading to incorrect e-challans (up to ₹10,000) for Karnataka drivers entering other states.

How to fix: Always carry a physical, stamped printout of your current PUCC when traveling inter-state. You can dispute incorrect e-challans online by uploading the valid physical PUCC copy to the parivahan challan grievance portal.

Thousands of licences are suspended annually due to reckless driving on highways (like Bengaluru-Mysuru), drinking and driving, or failure to wear helmets/seatbelts.

How to fix: Strict adherence to traffic laws is mandatory. If suspended, you must complete the suspension period, pay all fines, and sometimes undergo a refresher training program before reinstating your license.

Pressure to pay bribes to pass the driving test, and audits showing tests conducted simultaneously without proper supervision.

How to fix: Opt for RTOs equipped with Automated Driving Test Tracks (ADTT) in Bengaluru where evaluation is strictly sensor-based, eliminating the human bribery element.
